market_summary: Regime: mixed-to-risk-on. SPY/QQQ/IWM are all above MA50 and MA200, with IWM +3.70% 1m and QQQ +32.05% 3m; breadth improved beyond mega-cap tech, but leadership still skews to semicap/cyber.

Leadership: KLAC, PANW, and TSM remain the strongest pack-based winners; LLY and JPM add cleaner cross-sector momentum/quality than the current laggards. Gold and crypto trend remain weak, so no defensive metals or crypto sleeve is justified for a 30-day hold.

Go/no-go: rebalance is due, but not a high-conviction aggressive turnover setup. The cleanest upgrade is to remove the clearest thesis-damaged laggard (COST) for a higher-quality, above-MA50 momentum replacement (LLY) while keeping the rest of the winner stack intact.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows the weakest current-holding trend: 1m -2.18%, 3m -6.00%, momentum -1.32%, below MA50 and MA200. Relative trend vs SPY/QQQ is negative, and recurring 7-day context flagged COST repeatedly as the clearest laggard. Qualitative follow-up: UNKNOWN.
1m -11.94% versus SPY -1.03% and QQQ -0.15%, below MA50 with only modest 3m strength. Thesis not broken, but near-term factor support is weak. Qualitative follow-up: UNKNOWN.
1m -15.31%, vol20 72.87%, dd63 -24.08%, below MA50. Still above MA200 and 3m positive, so this is deterioration rather than full thesis failure. Qualitative follow-up: UNKNOWN.

thesis: Catalyst: LLY has strong recent leadership with 1m +8.55%, 3m +35.51%, above MA50/MA200, which aligns with the improved-but-still-selective risk-on tape.

Factor edge: this swap upgrades both momentum and quality mix versus COST, which has negative 3m momentum and sits below both MA50 and MA200; LLY also diversifies sector risk away from an overconcentrated tech/consumer mix.

Variant view: the market may be rotating toward durable earnings compounders outside semis, so adding LLY captures quality leadership before broader models fully reweight away from lagging defensives like COST.
risks: Macro: if the tape abruptly re-risks on lower-rate cyclicality or consumer defensives reassert leadership, LLY may lag despite strong current trend.
Idiosyncratic: LLY faces drug/regulatory/trial headline risk; COST could recover if defensive retail regains sponsorship.
Execution/liquidity: both are liquid large caps, so implementation risk is low, but chasing recent LLY strength after an 8.34% 1w move raises short-term pullback risk.
falsifiable_checks: Within 10 trading days, if LLY loses MA50 support or underperforms SPY by 5 percentage points or more on a 2-week basis, the replacement thesis is weakening.
By the next weekly checkpoint, if COST reclaims MA200 while LLY posts negative 1m relative momentum versus SPY, the swap edge should be reconsidered.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: regime is constructive but still selective, favoring names with both strong recent momentum and resilient quality characteristics. Bull: leadership broadens from semicap/cyber into healthcare compounders, making LLY a cleaner 30-day hold than COST. Bear: this is a late chase and defensives rebound, but COST's pack-based weakness is already persistent enough that the expected score improvement clears normal turnover drag for a single weekly paper change.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights -> KLAC/PANW/TSM remain top momentum leaders; LLY adds positive 1m/3m trend and sector diversification; COST ranked weakest among holdings. Qual/fundamentals -> NVDA verified high quality/valuation-rich (P/E 37.98, EV/EBITDA 31.54, op margin 65.6%, ROE 104.4%, rev growth 15.4%, earnings growth 21.0%); most other ratio fields UNKNOWN from qualitative refresh. Risk budget -> 68% IT, 13% Comm Services, 11% Cons Disc, 8% Health Care; no crypto; no single position >16%. Scenario stress -> equity index shock hurts all names, but health care sleeve modestly reduces pure tech beta; rates shock still mainly affects long-duration growth; commodity/USD shocks are secondary, with TSM macro/geopolitical sensitivity monitored. Execution plan -> one-for-one weekly paper rebalance only, staged mentally as single 8% swap, minimizing turnover. Monitoring/kill-switch -> review next checkpoint for MA50 breaks in LLY/TSM/PANW/KLAC, persistent underperformance in AMZN/AVGO/NVDA, and any verified negative guidance/revision headline; if two or more core leaders lose positive 30d/90d relative momentum vs SPY, reduce conviction materially.
